evidence: Working women's hostels were constructed in some districts, but not in all districts as committed.

evidence: Dr. Dharmambal widow remarriage scheme was increased from Rs 25,000 to Rs 30,000 with 8 grams gold; graduate scheme raised to Rs 60,000 with gold provision.

evidence: DMK could only 'urge' the central government as a state authority; no amendment was achieved, and the income ceiling for OBC creamy layer remains at Rs 8.5 lakh centrally.

evidence: No public announcement or construction commencement of Dravidian Movement Dheerargal Kottam has been verified as of 2025; project status remains unclear.
evidence_ta: Dravidian Movement Dheerargal Kottam kattavukkaga 2025 varai yaarukum ariyadha solli vilakal illai.

evidence: While statues of freedom fighters have been discussed, no confirmed completion of Pollan and Kuyili statues in Erode and Sivagangai has been publicly reported by 2025.
evidence_ta: Pollan mariyadhai sozhum pathai Erode-lum, Kuyili pathai Sivagangai-lum kattavukka sollirundhaalum, seiyakka vittadhu patri sollala ariyai.

evidence: No verified public reports of completed statues for Veerapandiya Kattabomman and Marudhu brothers in Chennai as of 2025.
evidence_ta: Veerapandiya Kattabomman mariyadhai-um Marudhu sodhalargal mariyadhai-um Chennai-la kattavukkaga vilakal illai.

evidence: No confirmed construction or inauguration of P Subbarayan memorial hall in Namakkal has been publicly announced or completed.
evidence_ta: P Subbarayan mariyadhai vilakal Namakkal-la kattavukka pannikkanum sollirundhaalum seiyakka vittadhu theriyala.

evidence: A statue of Vedhanayagam Pillai was installed in Mayiladuthurai, but completion status of archaeological museums from Keezhadi and other sites remains partial.
evidence_ta: Vedhanayagam Pillai pathai Mayiladuthurai-la kattavukkappattan, aanal Keezhadi-nukkana museums sampuram kattavukka pannikkum solladhu therivala.

evidence: Keezhadi archaeological museum project has progressed with artifact preservation, but museums at all promised locations (Adichanallur, Korkai) have not been fully completed.
evidence_ta: Keezhadi museum-kku sambandhappadhan adi vilakal nadandhuchu, aanal Adichanallur-um Korkai-yum-la museums sampuram pannikkappalai.

evidence: No public reports confirm the reclamation and restoration of Tamil inscriptions from the Archaeological Survey office in Mysore, Karnataka.
evidence_ta: Mysore-la Archaeological Survey office-la irundha Tamil vilakkangal adimai pannikkum sozhum pannakappattan therivala.

evidence: Disaster alert systems via SMS and television have been operational, though comprehensive coverage and effectiveness across all regions remains inconsistent.
evidence_ta: Kazhivukkana vilakal SMS-um TV-yum varai sollikkapatthuchu, aanal ellaam arangangalilum theriya pannalai.

evidence: Generator facilities have been provided to some police stations and hospitals, but implementation across all revenue offices during floods has been incomplete.
evidence_ta: Kela vitaikal police station-um hospital-um-kku generator pannikkappattan, aanal revenue office ellaam-kku vittavai theriyala.

evidence: Disaster management training initiatives have been undertaken, but evidence of a comprehensive sustained scheme for NGO and volunteer training is limited.
evidence_ta: Kazhivu manaippu training sollappattan, aanal NGO-um natpu makkalgalum-kku sampuram training scheme pannakappattan therivala.

evidence: Rural toilet construction continued under Swachh Bharat Mission, but complete eradication of open defecation in all Tamil Nadu villages has not been fully achieved.
evidence_ta: Swachh Bharat Mission varai toilet kattuvukkappattan, aanal Tamil Nadu ellaam vetti kudi kulirundhavai sampuram vittavai illai.

evidence: While rural development programs exist, a specifically named Tamil Nadu Rural Development Fund as promised has not been formally announced or established.
evidence_ta: Tamil Nadu Rural Development Fund keppatthukkappattan sollirundhaalum, avai sampuram sollikkapattan therivala.

evidence: Skill training programs for rural youth in plumbing and electrical work have been ongoing, but verification of specific cattle disease awareness initiatives is limited.
evidence_ta: Gramiya youth-kku skill training pannappattan, aanal karru kattikal karaipu nokkamkku manaippu pannappattan theriyala.

evidence: Infrastructure development in rural areas has continued, but comprehensive provision of all promised facilities (roads, electricity, medical centers, transport, water, schools) in remote villages remains incomplete.
evidence_ta: Gramiya infrastructure nadappattan, aanal malai melai kudi-yum, thozhilum-kku ellaam facilities pannakappattan illai.

evidence: Anna Marumalarchi Scheme has been re-implemented with some activity, but Namakku Naame scheme implementation and self-help group infrastructure activities have seen limited rollout.
evidence_ta: Anna Marumalarchi Scheme nadappattan, aanal Namakku Naame-yum self-help group vilaikal sampuram pannakappattan illai.

evidence: No evidence of state government launching a dedicated scheme to list construction materials as essential items with regulated fair pricing mechanism during 2021-2025 period.
evidence_ta: 2021-2025 kaalam la construction materials ku essential items status kodukkum scheme launch aana record illa.

evidence: DMK government reviewed guideline values through Registration Department in 2022-2023, reducing them in several districts, but complaints about high valuations continued in some areas, indicating incomplete resolution.
evidence_ta: 2022-2023 la guideline values kamikittaan samathiya, aanal some areas la high valuations continue panathukalam.

evidence: Single window clearance system was introduced with 60-day timeline for some categories, mandatory solar panels and rainwater harvesting implemented in guidelines, but implementation remained inconsistent across municipalities; fee reduction for blood relative transfers achieved.
evidence_ta: 60 days la single window system introduce panathanga, solar panels mandatory panathanga, aanal practical implementation consistent illa.

evidence: Expert committee formation status not publicly confirmed; no evidence of professional council for civil engineers established during 2021-2025 period.
evidence_ta: Expert committee form panuthaan nu clear proof illa, civil engineers council create aana record illa.

evidence: Online building approval system (e-APPARAM) was launched to reduce complexity for residential approvals, though full simplification across all land types remained incomplete.
evidence_ta: e-APPARAM system launch panathanga approval simple aana, full simplification complete illa.

evidence: Lawyer welfare fund was not increased from Rs 7 lakhs to Rs 10 lakhs; fund remains at similar previous levels without the promised enhancement.
evidence_ta: Lawyer welfare fund 7 lakhs la irundhaan, 10 lakhs ku increase aana record illa.

evidence: No concrete evidence of systematic office room construction for lawyers in district courts across Tamil Nadu during 2021-2025 period.
evidence_ta: District courts la lawyer offices construct pana record publicly available illa.

evidence: A dedicated fast-track court specifically for sexual crime cases against women was not established; instead, existing courts handle these with case prioritization in some districts.
evidence_ta: Sexual crimes ku separate court create aana record illa, existing courts la fast track process start pana.

evidence: Confidentiality guidelines for sexual crime survivors were strengthened through court directives and police procedures updated in 2022, though dedicated investigation sections were not uniformly created across all districts.
evidence_ta: Sexual crime survivors ku confidentiality strengthen panathanga, aanal separate investigation sections all districts la create aana record illa.

evidence: Tamil Nadu Ex-Servicemen Board was activated to address grievances, but priority employment for retired personnel implementation remained limited with few concrete job placements reported.
evidence_ta: Ex-Servicemen Board activate panathanga, priority jobs scheme announce pana, aanal actual implementation kashtam.

evidence: Veterinary hospitals expanded in some districts with ambulance services initiated, but coverage remained incomplete with Blue Cross ambulance service not fully operational in all required locations.
evidence_ta: Veterinary hospitals create panathanga, ambulance service partial la start pana, all panchayats la full service illa.

evidence: Veterinary camps were conducted sporadically in village panchayats, particularly through health department initiatives, but not systematically in all panchayats as promised.
evidence_ta: Veterinary camps some panchayats la conduct panathanga, but all panchayats la regular camps conduct aana record illa.

evidence: No systematic compensation scheme for cattle deaths from road accidents was implemented; affected farmers faced continued lack of official financial relief mechanism.
evidence_ta: Road accidents la cattle maran compensation scheme implement aana record illa.

evidence: Rajapalayam and Kangayam cattle breeding programs were supported through veterinary department initiatives, but systematic conservation of all three breeds (including Chippiparai) remained incomplete.
evidence_ta: Kangayam cattle breeding program support panathanga, Chippiparai breed systematic la conserve aana record less.

evidence: No evidence of state-wide health card issuance system for pet animals and livestock implemented during 2021-2025 period.
evidence_ta: Pet animals ku health cards issue pana scheme launch aana record publicly available illa.

evidence: CCTV installation initiated in schools and public places but implementation remains incomplete across all specified locations; false case withdrawal process started but not comprehensively completed as promised.
evidence_ta: Schools ah CCTVs podanum nu start pannaanga, but police stations, temples la full ah complete aagalaye; false cases withdraw pannanum nu sonnaanga, but all cases withdraw aagalaye.

evidence: Some cases against Sterlite and Kudankulam protesters have been withdrawn, but comprehensive withdrawal of all false cases from all four projects mentioned has not been completed.
evidence_ta: Sterlite ku virudha protest case konjam withdraw pannaanga, but all cases withdraw aagalaye.

evidence: TNPSC malpractices were acknowledged and investigations initiated, but comprehensive inquiry findings and punishments of guilty officials have not been decisively concluded.
evidence_ta: TNPSC malpractice issue investigate pannanum nu sonnanga, but guilty officials ku punishment clear ah aagalaye.

evidence: Transparency measures like online application portals were introduced for TNPSC and TRB recruitment, though critics argue full transparency in selection process remains incomplete.
evidence_ta: Online application system introduce pannaanga transparency ku, but full transparency implementation still pending.

evidence: Tamil Nadu government increased property tax rates in 2022-2023 despite Covid economic impact continuing, directly contradicting the promise not to hike taxes.
evidence_ta: Covid effect still irukum podhu property tax hike pannunaanga, promise break pannunaanga.

evidence: DMK government's stance on railway privatisation is largely dependent on central government actions; state-level opposition voiced in legislative forums but limited state-level control over railway policy.
evidence_ta: Railway privatisation oppose pannanum nu statement vara, but state government kku railroad policy control illaye.

evidence: DMK expressed opposition to banking sector privatisation in government statements and legislative discussions, but the issue remains primarily a central government matter beyond state control.
evidence_ta: Bank privatisation against statement pannunaanga, but central government issue itu, state kku control illaye.

evidence: DMK government opposed LIC privatisation through statements and legislative positions, but the outcome depends on central government policy decisions beyond state authority.
evidence_ta: LIC privatisation against stand pannunaanga, but centre decide pannum issue itu.

evidence: Ministry for New Schemes Implementation was created; 100-day report and press conference occurred; however, systematic monthly reviews and comprehensive complaint resolution from election campaign remain inconsistent.
evidence_ta: Ministry create pannaanga, 100 days report pannunaanga, but monthly reviews consistent ah continue aagalaye.

evidence: Religious institutions have received approvals for construction on their lands, but no specific legislation formalising unrestricted religious centre approval irrespective of affiliation has been comprehensively enacted.
evidence_ta: Religious institutions ku approval vara, but formal law pass aagalaye.

evidence: Despite government efforts, Tamil language teaching mandate in Kendriya Vidyalayas was not legislated as they fall under central government jurisdiction; state schools show increased Tamil but without comprehensive legislation.
evidence_ta: Kendriya Vidyalayas centre government control la irukum, state kku change panna mudiyalaye.

evidence: Perarivalan was released in 2022 on humanitarian grounds after Governor's remission, but remaining six convicts' release has not been completed despite government efforts.
evidence_ta: Perarivalan release pannunaanga, but baaki 6 per release aagalaye.

evidence: 100% job allocation to Tamil Nadu youth could not be implemented due to legal constraints under Indian Constitution's Articles 16 and 35; recruitment still follows national norms.
evidence_ta: Constitution rule irukum, 100% Tamil Nadu youth job allocation possible illaye.

evidence: New university proposals for Kanchipuram and Erode have been announced and some preliminary steps taken, but full establishment with operational campuses remains incomplete.
evidence_ta: University establish pannum proposal vera, but full ah operational aagalaye.

evidence: Free bus travel scheme for senior citizens aged 70+ was successfully launched and implemented across Tamil Nadu transport corporation in 2021, becoming operational statewide.
evidence_ta: 70 years age ku mela unnavan ku free bus travel scheme launch pannaanga, state la running.

evidence: DMK government did not successfully persuade the Central Government to amend the Citizenship Act 2019 to include Sri Lanka; no legislative change occurred despite representations.
evidence_ta: DMK government ah central government-kah uraikku Sri Lanka-va 2019 Citizenship Act-la add panra murai illa; ethaiyum nadankala.

evidence: No new LNG pipelines were laid connecting Ennore to other power houses for 2000 MW electricity generation during the 2021-2025 term.
evidence_ta: Ennore LNG factory-irundhu pipelines podu panra vishyam nadankala; 2000 MW electricity vettai ethanu fulfill aana.

evidence: Central Government did not amend the Citizenship Act 2019 to include Sri Lanka; no pathway to citizenship was created for Sri Lankan refugees in camps.
evidence_ta: Central Government eh Citizenship Act-la Sri Lanka add pannala; refugee camps-la irukka manushan-kah citizenship koduppan illa.

evidence: DMK government implemented free bus travel for women in town buses (SRTC urban services) from June 2021 onwards as a flagship welfare scheme.
evidence_ta: Ponna-kah town bus-la free travel koduppa pannathu; 2021-irundhu start aichuthu.

evidence: DMK distributed ₹4,000 cash stimulus to ration cardholders but not consistently from June 2021; cooking gas cylinder subsidy of ₹100 was implemented with delayed/irregular disbursement.
evidence_ta: 4000 rupees ah ration card holder-kah koduthanga aana regular ala illa; gas cylinder subsidy ah delay-la koduthanga.

evidence: ₹100 subsidy on cooking gas cylinders for ration cardholders was announced and partially implemented but faced distribution delays and irregular availability.
evidence_ta: Gas cylinder subsidy 100 rupees ah koduthanga aana regular ala continuous distribution illa.

evidence: State government cannot unilaterally reduce petrol and diesel prices as these are controlled by Central Government; DMK made no effective moves to reduce fuel prices by promised amounts.
evidence_ta: Petrol diesel price reduce panra power state government-ukku illa; central government control-la irukku.

evidence: Aavin milk price was not reduced from ₹6 to ₹3 per litre; instead prices increased or remained stagnant during the DMK tenure due to operational and subsidy constraints.
evidence_ta: Aavin milk price 6 rupees-irundhu 3 rupees-ukku reduce aana; instead price increase thaan nadanthuthu.
